items.title: Thirteen years after shark hooks, WA still grapples with deadly attacks

children.content: The approach to tackling shark attacks has changed dramatically since the 2013 catch-and-kill policy, with a focus on shark tagging and tracking. But it's never enough to stop fatal attacks.

articles.title: 'Devoted father and gentleman': Tributes flow for shark attack victim

children.content: The wife of 38-year-old Perth man Steven Mattaboni says her family's "hearts are irrevocably broken" after he was killed in a shark attack while spearfishing off the coast of Rottnest Island on Saturday.

image.altText: A man holding a little girl and a woman holding a baby with the beach in the background

articles.title: Friends witness 'horrific' shark attack that claimed life of spearfishing mate

children.content: A 38-year-old man had been spearfishing with friends when he was killed in a shark attack off Rottnest Island on Saturday morning.

image.altText: Police and ambulance parked near a jetty at a bay

gallery.altText: A great white shark under water in the deep blue sea.
A man on a boat holding a large fish.

thumbnail.altText: A great white shark under water in the deep blue sea.

items.title: Stalking accusations against WA police assistant commissioner, trial begins

image.altText: A woman with short hair and wearing a white top leaves the court after a trial.

children.content: A court hears WA police assistant commissioner Gailene Hamilton allegedly made a female subordinate officer feel "smothered" and used a restricted access computer system to check on her location.

items.title: Stronger mouse poison allowed for 'worst plague on record'

children.content: Farm groups have welcomed the chemical regulator's long-awaited decision to approve double-strength mouse baits amid plague numbers in Western Australia and South Australia.

image.altText: Three mice in farm shed.

items.title: Federal government intervenes for third time over China-linked stakes in rare earths miner

children.content: The federal treasurer orders another six companies to divest their holdings in Northern Minerals as concerns grow in Canberra that Chinese-backed investors are trying to seize control of the rare earths mining company.

image.altText: A sign explaining a new mine sits in red dirt as a person walks past in boots

items.title: 'Bro, there's a frog in my lettuce': Woolworths investigates salad bag surprise

children.content: A Kiwi couple in the town of Esperance on WA's south coast share an unlikely discovery, a live frog in a bag of Woolworths lettuce.

image.altText: A small frog in a bag of lettuce

gallery.altText: A frog inside a plastic bag with lettuce on a table
A green frog sitting on leaves

A green frog sitting on leaves

items.title: PM stands by budget amid poll dip

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese joined Oliver Peterson on ABC Radio Perth to sell the budget and explain why it's a "really responsible budget".

image.altText: Anthony Albanese ABC Perth studios

items.title: Opposition Leader claims Coalition budget plan will 'restore standard of living'

children.content: Listen back as Opposition Leader Angus Taylor shares vision for budget with Oly Peterson on ABC Radio Perth.

image.altText: Angus Taylor speaking to media standing outside a building.

items.title: Bid to reopen Albany hydrotherapy pool rejected

children.content: The WA health minister rejects recommissioning Albany's public hydrotherapy pool despite a feasibility study saying there is a justifiable cause to do so.

gallery.altText: Older women standing in her lush garden using a walking frame

thumbnail.altText: Older women standing in her lush garden using a walking frame

items.title: 'I wish I were dead': The heartbreaking toll of Australia's favourite pastime

children.content: Australians are the world's biggest gamblers, losing billions every year. But the true cost goes far beyond the financial.

image.altText: An illustration of a silhouetted man sitting at a colourful roulette table.

items.title: Labor flags relief for small businesses to adjust to trust tax changes

children.content: The federal government flags relief for small businesses that will need to adjust to trust tax changes announced in last week's budget. Follow live.

image.altText: mark butler press club speech

items.title: 'Two years to live': Why we used our super for weight loss surgery

children.content: Weight loss surgery has become an increasingly common option for people grappling with obesity — and many are funding it using retirement savings.

image.altText: A man with glasses, a white t-shirt and a sleeve tattoo, stands beside a woman with glasses and a dark blue top.

items.title: What Kylie Minogue learned from Michael Hutchence and Nick Cave

children.content: Australia's reigning Queen of Pop discusses her relationships with Nick Cave and the late Michael Hutchence, and being tapped to headline the AFL grand final's pre-game entertainment.

image.altText: pop star Kylie Minogue sits in a dressing gown on a deck chair smiling pensively.

items.title: Marine life bounces back in Swan River after reefs restored

children.content: Work is underway to restore Perth's Swan River and help its severely degraded riverbed, where 1 million tonnes of limestone was taken in the 1990s.

gallery.altText: Man in black puffer with bushland in the background
An excavator on a boat shovelling limestone bricks into the Swan River.

thumbnail.altText: Man in black puffer with bushland in the background
An excavator on a boat shovelling limestone bricks into the Swan River.

items.title: Six shops ordered to shut under new illegal tobacco laws

children.content: More than 140,000 illegal cigarettes have been seized and six stores ordered to close down, days after Western Australia's new tobacco laws came into force.

image.altText: A shop front with 'store closed' banners stuck on the front windows

items.title: Today's quiz tests you on iconic movie quotes. You. Shall. (Probably.) Pass!

children.content: The ABC Timelines quiz is back to test your knowledge of some of the most iconic movie quotes of the 2000s.

items.title: Tourism operators priced out of own industry conference

children.content: Struggling businesses in Carnarvon, reliant on tourism, say they want more support to be involved with the 2026 WA Tourism Conference that the region is hosting.

items.title: What you can do today to ease money stress in your relationship

children.content: While financial tension between partners isn't always about money, many people clash over values, spending habits and how they respond to financial pressure.

image.altText: family in kitchen with baby and child

items.title: Boomers star Joe Ingles thrilled to come 'home' in coup NBL signing

children.content: The legendary Boomers and NBA star will return to Australia's NBL for the first time since 2009, signing with Melbourne United for two years.

image.altText: Joe Ingles smiles with a towel around his neck

items.title: Teenage pedestrian dies after being hit in Perth car park

children.content: A 19-year-old driver is being questioned by police after a teenager is struck by a car in a Perth shopping centre car park and dies in hospital.

image.altText: A car being towed away in a car park

items.title: Overseas company makes billions in profit but is receiving millions in government bailouts

children.content: China is "playing a hard game" with its stranglehold on the global minerals and metals market and Australian smelters need taxpayer support to "play hard back".

image.altText: Two large white buildings at a metal smelter by a port.

items.title: Cakes and connection as cafe project gives women confidence boost

children.content: Beyond the Kitchen, a course run by Multicultural Services Centre of WA, gives women from migrant, refugee and asylum seeker backgrounds a safe space to develop friendships and hospitality business skills.

items.title: There's no cure and no treatment for MND, but finally there is hope

children.content: Natalie has seen her body and speech deteriorate; she has lost her independence and dignity — but she says new research into motor neurone disease has given her hope for those diagnosed in the future.

image.altText: Natalie Verdouw

items.title: Cummins stars as Sunrisers Hyderabad book spot in IPL playoffs

children.content: Pat Cummins delivers when his team needs it most with crucial wickets as Sunrisers Hyderabad beat Chennai Super Kings.

image.altText: Pat Cummins celebrates a wicket with teammate

items.title: WA's first pair of fainting goats settle into new home

children.content: The goats, which "faint" when stressed or excited, have travelled to a farm near Busselton, where they will be bred as pets.
